products.title: Everyday Tee — Ecru
products.description: Our signature everyday tee, cut from a breathable 180gsm single jersey. 100% GOTS-certified organic cotton grown without synthetic pesticides. Garment-dyed with low-impact fibre-reactive dyes. Pre-shrunk, side-seamed. Machine wash cold, line dry. Woven, cut and sewn in a Fair Wear audited unit.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 100% Organic cotton — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Certifications
factors.detail: GOTS (+6), Fair Wear Foundation (+3).

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

sustainability.explanation: This tee is genuinely strong on fibre and labor sourcing: 100% GOTS-certified organic cotton and Fair Wear Foundation audited manufacturing are verifiable, meaningful credentials. The dyeing process is described only as 'low-impact' without any certification or specific standard, so that claim can't be independently verified.
greenwash_flags: 'low-impact fibre-reactive dyes' is a vague environmental claim with no certification backing

products.title: Everyday Tee — Moss

The 5% elastane blend slightly limits recyclability and biodegradability, and no certification covers the elastane content itself.

There are no vague or unsubstantiated claims in the copy, though the brand doesn't mention water use or dye-fixing chemicals, so the full environmental footprint of the natural dye process isn't fully disclosed.

sustainability.explanation: The apron uses organic cotton canvas and a PFAS-free natural beeswax finish, which are genuine, verifiable material choices, and the brand backs its labor practices with SA8000 factory audits and a lifetime repair guarantee. However, there's no certification (e.g., GOTS) confirming the organic cotton claim, and the 'renewable, not disposable' finish language is marketing flourish rather than a substantiated fact.
greenwash_flags: 'the finish is renewable, not disposable' — vague durability claim with no supporting certification or data

products.title: Timber Flannel Shirt
products.description: Brushed both sides for warmth. 100% organic cotton flannel, 200gsm, yarn-dyed check so the pattern never fades. OCS certified, SA8000-audited stitching unit. Cut roomy for layering.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 100% Organic cotton — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

factors.label: Repair programme
factors.detail: Brand repairs the garment, extending its life.

sustainability.explanation: This shirt uses 100% organic cotton with OCS certification, backing its organic fibre claim, and SA8000 audits address labor conditions in the stitching unit. The lifetime repair guarantee (from brand context) extends garment life, though no dye or water-use details are given, and 'yarn-dyed so it never fades' is a durability claim rather than a sustainability one.

products.title: Deadstock Wool Blazer
products.description: One of 40, ever. Cut from rescued deadstock suiting — 85% virgin wool / 15% polyamide — sourced from an Italian mill's unsold rolls. Using existing fabric means zero new fibre production for this piece. Half-canvas construction, made to order in 14 days.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 85% Virgin wool, 15% Nylon — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

factors.label: Deadstock / rescued fabric
factors.detail: Uses existing fabric — zero new fibre production for this piece.

factors.label: Made to order
factors.detail: Nothing is produced unless sold — no overstock landfill.

sustainability.explanation: Using rescued deadstock fabric genuinely avoids new fibre production and the made-to-order model limits overproduction waste, both concrete and verifiable practices. However, the blazer contains virgin wool and conventional polyamide with no certifications (no RWS, no recycled content, no OEKO-TEX) verifying animal welfare, land use, or chemical safety standards, so its overall environmental footprint beyond the fabric-sourcing story remains unverified.

products.title: Air Blouse
products.description: Feather-light and fluid. 100% TENCEL Lyocell satin, 120gsm. Made to order — we cut only what's sold, so nothing ends up landfilled. Shell buttons. Cool iron if needed.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 100% TENCEL / Lyocell — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

factors.label: Made to order
factors.detail: Nothing is produced unless sold — no overstock landfill.

sustainability.explanation: This blouse is made from a single fiber, TENCEL Lyocell, a cellulosic fabric generally produced with lower water and chemical use than conventional viscose, though no Lenzing or other certification is cited here to verify sourcing. The made-to-order model is a genuine plus, cutting fabric only after a sale to reduce overproduction waste. Weak points: no certifications are listed for the fabric or production process, so claims about sustainability rest on the brand's word rather than third-party verification.

products.title: Eaux Slip Dress
products.description: Evening ease. Deadstock viscose satin (rescued from a closed fashion house) — 100% viscose. Bias cut, adjustable straps. Each colourway limited to the fabric we could rescue; when it's gone, it's gone.

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

factors.label: Deadstock / rescued fabric
factors.detail: Uses existing fabric — zero new fibre production for this piece.

sustainability.explanation: This dress uses rescued deadstock viscose satin, which is a genuinely sustainable choice since it repurposes fabric that would otherwise go to waste and avoids new resource extraction. However, viscose production itself is often chemically intensive and no certification (like FSC for viscose sourcing or OEKO-TEX for dye safety) is mentioned to verify the environmental standards of this specific fabric.

products.title: Circuit Run Tee
products.description: Race-day light at 110gsm. 100% recycled polyester (GRS certified) from post-consumer bottles — 6 bottles per tee. Wicks fast, dries faster. Ships with a Guppyfriend washing bag to catch microfibres. OEKO-TEX certified.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 100% Recycled polyester — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Certifications
factors.detail: GRS (+4), OEKO-TEX Standard 100 (+2), USDA Organic (+4), RWS (+3), OCS (+3), OEKO-TEX Standard 100 (+2), FSC (+2), OEKO-TEX Standard 100 (+2) — capped at 15.

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

sustainability.explanation: This tee uses 100% GRS-certified recycled polyester and is OEKO-TEX certified, backing its sustainability claims with real certifications rather than vague language. Shipping with a Guppyfriend bag and publishing microfibre-shedding data shows genuine attention to plastic pollution, though the fabric is still synthetic and will shed some microplastics regardless.

products.title: Flow Leggings
products.description: Squat-proof, planet-kinder. 78% ECONYL® regenerated nylon (from recovered fishing nets and carpet waste) / 22% elastane. Four-way stretch, high compression, hidden waistband pocket. We publish our microfibre shedding test results — these shed 40% less than virgin nylon equivalents.

factors.label: Fibre composition
factors.detail: 78% Recycled nylon, 22% Elastane — weighted fibre impact (max 70).

factors.label: Brand practices
factors.detail: Brand-level transparency, labour and supply-chain record.

sustainability.explanation: The leggings use a high proportion (78%) of ECONYL, a genuinely recycled nylon made from ocean-bound waste, and the brand backs up its shedding claims with published third-party-style test data rather than vague promises. However, the 22% elastane is virgin synthetic, no certifications (like GRS) are cited to verify the recycled content, and the phrase 'planet-kinder' is unsubstantiated marketing language.
greenwash_flags: 'planet-kinder' — vague sustainability claim with no certification backing it

products.title: Reef Swim Shorts

However, elastane is not recyclable or biodegradable, and the product copy itself doesn't mention any additional certifications, dyeing practices, or end-of-life programs for this specific item.

products.title: Ridgeline Trail Jacket
products.description: Wind-shedding trail shell. 100% ECONYL regenerated nylon ripstop with PFC-free DWR. Packs to fist-size. Every zip, cord and toggle is recycled or recyclable — designed for single-material recycling at end of life.

However, no third-party certification (like GRS) is cited to verify the recycled content, and the claim that trims are 'recycled or recyclable' is vague without evidence of an actual take-back or recycling program in place.

products.title: Crew Tee 3-Pack
products.description: Three everyday crew necks at an honest price. Soft-touch eco-friendly fabric: 60% cotton (Better Cotton Initiative) / 40% polyester. 160gsm. Tagless neck, ribbed collar. Machine wash warm.

sustainability.explanation: The BCI cotton portion reflects a real, verifiable sourcing improvement over conventional cotton, but the 40% conventional polyester blend adds virgin fossil-fuel-based fiber and makes the garment harder to recycle. The 'eco-friendly fabric' language oversells the product since only part of the blend has any sustainability backing, and no other certifications, dyeing practices, or end-of-life programs are mentioned.
greenwash_flags: 'Soft-touch eco-friendly fabric' is a vague claim not substantiated beyond the BCI cotton content

products.title: Comfy Jogger

However, the garment is 35% virgin polyester, a fossil-fuel-based synthetic with no recycled content, and the phrase 'sustainably minded' is unsubstantiated marketing language with no certification or concrete practice to back it up.

products.title: Easy Oxford Shirt

However, BCI is not a strict certification like GOTS and doesn't guarantee organic or fully traceable production, and the brand's broader sustainability transparency is limited.

products.title: Everyday Hoodie
products.description: Green in colour, greener in spirit. Eco-blend fleece: 55% BCI cotton / 45% polyester, 300gsm. Double-lined hood, kangaroo pocket. Planet-conscious comfort for less.

factors.label: Fibre composition

The copy leans heavily on vague, feel-good phrases like 'eco-blend' and 'planet-conscious' without any certification or specific data to back them up, which is a clear greenwashing pattern. No dyeing practices, repair programs, or end-of-life initiatives are mentioned, reflecting the brand's early-stage and low-transparency sustainability approach.

Eco-blend fleece
Planet-conscious comfort for less
